title: Bridge Essay: From computers to cultivation: reconceptualizing evolutionary psychology.
concept: Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
**Phase context:** Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

**From computers to cultivation: reconceptualizing evolutionary psychology.** — Front Psychol (2014). DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2014.00867. PMID: 25161633. Does evolutionary theorizing have a role in psychology? This is a more contentious issue than one might imagine, given that, as evolved creatures, the answer must surely be yes. The contested nature of evolutionary psychology lies not in our status as evolved beings, but in the extent to which evolutionary ideas add value to studies of human behavior, and the rigor with which these ideas are tested. This, in turn, is linked to the framework in which particular evolutionary ideas are situated. Wh
